project_overview.project_name: Djibouti Emergency Food Security Crisis Response Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To contribute to mitigating food security risks posed by food supply shocks and drought
project_overview.beneficiaries: Entire population of Djibouti (particularly poorest and vulnerable including refugees), 8,000 drought-affected households (50,000 people), farmers and livestock herders

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - country hosts 35,000 internally displaced people including 23,000 refugees and 11,000 asylum seekers; affected by conflict in neighboring Ethiopia

key_components.component_name: Mitigating food security risks posed by food supply shocks
key_components.description: Establish emergency food stock, Market Analysis Unit, Food Shortage Preparedness Fund, and capacity building for risk management

key_components.component_name: Mitigating food security risks posed by drought
key_components.description: Improve water access, restore agricultural and pastoral livelihoods, establish drought warning system

key_components.component_name: Project management and preparation of preparedness plan
key_components.description: Project implementation unit, monitoring and evaluation, Food Security Preparedness Plan development

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Social Affairs and Solidarity (MASS)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee led by MASS, MOUs with Ministry of Commerce and Tourism (MCT) and Ministry of Agriculture (MAEPE-RH)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Framework Agreements with private importers/logistics companies, World Bank procurement regulations with emergency procedures
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Project Implementation Unit with M&E specialist, semi-annual progress reports, mid-term review, beneficiary satisfaction surveys

identified_risks.risk_description: High volatility of food commodity markets, integrity risks in urgent procurement, food stock management and distribution risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Framework Agreements with clear obligations, technical audits, citizen surveys, strong internal controls for storage

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited implementation capacity, new activities outside expertise, coordination challenges between ministries
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical assistance and capacity building, MOUs between agencies, enhanced Bank implementation support

identified_risks.risk_description: Combined effects of Ukraine war, COVID-19, Ethiopia conflict, and drought on fiscal and external accounts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Emergency food stock and preparedness fund, private sector engagement, government commitment to National Solidarity Fund

identified_risks.risk_description: Food storage/transport risks, livestock disease introduction, elite capture, waste generation from agricultural activities
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Updated Environmental and Social Management Framework, site-specific ESMPs, health risk controls for livestock imports

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level grievance mechanism to be established with specialist in PIU
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: 30% women representation in regional committees, 60% of small ruminants to women-headed households, priority to women-headed households for inputs

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project addresses impacts of regional conflicts (Ethiopia, Somalia) on food security and trade disruptions; serves refugee populations
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Country affected by multiple crises: Ukraine war impacts, COVID-19, Ethiopia conflict, severe drought; hosts significant refugee population in unstable Horn of Africa region
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Food riots risk due to price increases; regional instability affecting trade routes and economic activity; potential for social unrest
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - hosts 35,000 internally displaced including 23,000 refugees and 11,000 asylum seekers; 8% increase in refugee influx since January 2021; emergency food stock will serve refugees
